observation: “Abstract of the Factories Act, 1948 and The Gujarat factories Rules, 1963” (Form No. 23) was not displayed in a conspicuous and convenient place at or near the main entrance of the factory.
recommendation: As per under Rule 106 of The Gujarat Factories Rules (GFR), 1963, “Abstract of the Factories Act, 1948 and The Gujarat factories Rules, 1963” (Form No. 23) should be displayed in a conspicuous and convenient place at or near the main entrance of the factory in prominent manner.

observation: First aid box available across the factory but copy of FIRST-AID LEAFLET (Situations-Procedures) issued by the Directorate general of Factories Advice Service and labour Institutes, Government of India, Mumbai was not evident in all First Aid Boxes.
recommendation: As per the Rule 70 (C) (xxiii) of The GFR, 1963, one Copy of first aid leaflet issued by the Directorate general of Factories Advice Service and labour Institutes, Government of India, Mumbai shall be kept in each first aid box.

observation: Section 111 A Safety Training records was not available.
recommendation: Safety Training as required under Section 111 A (ii) shall be provided to all employees by Government

observation: Record of undertaking taken from employee for availing training on Precaution, Hazard, Precautionary notice and instruction was not evident.
recommendation: As per Part 2 (4) of Schedule XIX of rule 102 of the GFR, 1963 An undertaking from the workers shall be obtained within 1 month of their employment and for old workers employed within one month of coming in to operation of these rules, to the effect that they have read the contents of the cautionary notices and instruments, understood by them and would abide by them.

observation: Safety shower:
First aid box available in the factory but notice regarding first aid was missing.
recommendation: As per Rule 70 A of The Gujarat Factories Rules (GFR), 1963, A notice containing the names of the persons working within the premises of the factory who are trained in first aid treatment and who are in charge of the first aid boxes or cupboards shall be posted in every factory at a conspicuous place and

observation: Siren code was missing in entire plant premises.
recommendation: Siren code would be displayed in prominent manner and in local language in entire plant premises as suggested below.

observation: SOP/Do’s & Don’ts not displayed at all operation areas and Chemical storage area.
recommendation: SOP/Do’s & Don’ts to be displayed at all operation areas and Chemical storage area.

observation: Confined space entry was not identified and not displayed.
recommendation: As per Rule 102, Schedule 19, Part II, Para (18) (1) (a) of The Gujarat Factories Rules, 1963, Confined pace entry to be identified and to be displayed.

observation: Logbook of every entry into or work in confined spaces was not evident.
recommendation: As per Rule 102, SCHEDULE 19, PART II, (18) of The Gujarat Factories Rules, 1963, The manager shall maintain a log book of every entry into or work in, confined spaces and such record shall contain the details of persons assigned for the work, the location of the work and such other details that would have a bearing on the safety and health of the persons assigned for this work. The log book so maintained shall be retained as long as the concerned workers are in service and produced to the Inspector when demanded.

observation: Maximum Permissible Working Pressure (MPWP) was not marked on Pressure Gauge (PG) in Plant.
recommendation: As per Rule 61 (4) (b) of Gujarat Factories Rules 1963, MPWP shall be marked with prominent red colour on Pressure Gauge.

observation: Safe Working Load (SWL) of material hoist/lift was not displayed.
recommendation: As per the Section 28 (c) of The Factories Act 1963, the maximum safe working load shall be plainly marked on every hoist/lift, and no load greater than such load shall be carried thereon.

observation: Emergency escape route with arrow and symbols were not available in operation area.
recommendation: Paint or fix sticker for Emergency escape route with arrow & symbols, which can be easily seen from a working area and at junction where there is more than one way- would be displayed as suggested below.

observation: Emergency Exit signage was displayed at few locations.
recommendation: As per DISH notification no. DISH/H-Safety/2019/958 to 1057 Dated: 24/09/2019.

observation: PPE matrix display was not found in plant area & Usage of PPEs found limited
recommendation: Prepare PPE matrix to use specific PPE on the bases of operation / risk / hazard. Give training to employees, and instruct to use PPE as decided in PPE matrix during the activity.

observation: Mock drill records as per Rule 68-J not evident.
recommendation: As per Rule 68-J (12) (4) (5) of The Gujarat Factories Rules, 1963 The occupier shall ensure that a mock drill of the on-site emergency is conducted at least once in every six months and reports of the shall be made available to DISH.

observation: Factory handling hazardous substances however formal system for participation of workers in health and safety found inadequate.
recommendation: As per Section 41 – G of The Factories Act, 1948 and there under Rule 68 – Y of The Gujarat Factories Rules, 1963 in every factory carrying out hazardous processes or handling hazardous

observation: Work Permit System was evident but found inadequate.
recommendation: Work Permit System (WPS) to be strengthen. WPS form to be updated and effective JSA to be performed.

observation: Trained first aider was not available
recommendation: As per section 45, Para III of the Factory Act 1948, First aider should obtain certificate in first aid treatment recognized by State Government Institute.

observation: HAZOP study was not evident for process plant.
recommendation: HAZOP study shall be carried out for all process plant to identify potential hazard and operability issues

observation: ERT Team/First Aider/ Fire frightening team was not available in factory.
recommendation: The occupier shall arrange to have 10 % ten percent of the workers trained in the use of first aid, fire-fighting appliances and in the rendering of specific first aid measures taking- into consideration the special hazards of the particular process -- schedule 19, rule 102

observation: Factory handling hazardous substances however records of workplace monitoring was not evident.
recommendation: As per the Section 7-A of The Factories Act, 1948, every occupier shall ensure to carry out workplace monitoring and records shall be maintained Form No. 37 as prescribed under Rule 12 – B of The Gujarat Factories Rules, 1963.

observation: Pre-employment medical check-up of employee and worker was done but certificate of fitness in Form No. 33 was not evident.
recommendation: As per Rule 68 T (2) of The Gujarat Factories Rules, 1963 No person shall be employed for the first time without a certificate of fitness in Form No. 33. certificate of fitness in Form No. 33 shall be maintained.

observation: Fire drill records was not evident.
recommendation: As per Rule 66-A (12) (c) of The Gujarat Factories, 1963 Firefighting drill shall be held as often as necessary and at least once in every period of 2 months and records maintained.

observation: Identification no., Resistance value, last test date, next due test date was missing on earth pit.
recommendation: Resistance value, last test date, next due test date should be displayed on each earth pit.

observation: Mechanical guarding of rotating parts of machines at many places found inadequate;
recommendation: As per the Section 21 of The Factories Act, 1948 all rotating parts of machines shall be adequately guarded to restrict the accessibility while in motion. It is recommended to verify all machines for adequacy of the existing guarding and rectify on high priority.

observation: Shock treatment chart in English or Hindi and local language was not display in all panel room.
recommendation: Shock treatment chart in English or Hindi and local language should be display in all panel room.

observation: Display board of hazardous waste was not found at entrance of factory.
recommendation: As per supreme court order no. W.P. No. 657 of 1995 Dated: 14/10/2003, Display board of hazardous waste should be updated on regular basis. Updated board as per valid GPCB consent.

observation: Hazard Identification & Risk Assessment (HIRA) was not evident.
recommendation: As per Rule 68-0 (4) (e) of The Gujarat Factories Rules, 1963 Hazard Identification & Risk assessment (HIRA) to be prepared and regularly update at every 2 years.

observation: Hazardous Area Classification (HAC) study was not available.
recommendation: As per Schedule XIX Part-III Paragraph under Rule 102 of The Gujarat factories Rules, 1963 Hazardous Area Classification shall be done and accordingly electrical equipment shall be installed.

observation: Workmen / employees handling hazardous substances were not adequately aware about the type of substances stored / handled, hazards of substances, their hazardous properties, exposure consequences, control measures, personal protective equipment to be used etc.
recommendation: As per Section 41 B / C / H of The Factories Act, 1948 and Rule thereunder i.e., Rule 68-K of The Gujarat Factories Rules, 1963 The occupier of the factory involving a hazardous process shall supply information to all workers individually through booklets or leaflets and display of cautionary notice at workplaces.

observation: Guard to pump coupling, agitator coupling & blower or agitator belt or chain was not found in most the cases.
recommendation: This is a statutory requirement to cover all rotating parts to prevent from body injury.

observation: Safety shower provided in plant area where acid / Alkyl / liquid
recommendation: It is recommended to display notice as per GFR as below chemicals were handled but there was not display of cautionary board found near safety shower.

observation: Periodical medical examination was carried out and records maintained in form-32, but employees details was not complete filled and entire units employees were covered in a single register i.e. no any identification or segregation for other units like unit-2, 3, etc.
recommendation: Employees adequate details to be filled in form-32. If possible separate register to be prepared for all the units or need proper identification / segregation for each unit.

observation: Illumination study records was not evident.
recommendation: As per sec-17 of The Factories Act-1948 & as per rule-31 of the Gujarat Factories Rules-1963, in every part of the factory where workers are working or passing there shall be provided and maintained sufficient and suitable lighting, natural or artificial, or both. Illumination study to be carried out for adequacy of the lighting facility.

observation: QRA study was not evident
recommendation: QRA study to be carried out for consequence analysis.

observation: On site emergency plan (OEP) was available but not updated.
recommendation: On site emergency plan (OSEP) should be prepared as per Rule 68-J, Schedule 8 A of The Gujarat factories Rules, (GFR) 1963.

observation: Factory handling hazardous substances but Self-contained Breathing Apparatus (SCBA) set not available for rescue or handling toxic release.
recommendation: It is recommended to provide SCBA set in plant to reduce the response time in case of emergency.

observation: a) Piping color-coding chart display not found in operational area and name of pipeline chemical content not marked on all pipelines, this might result wrong chemical charging or isolation.
Over all housekeeping not to the required level, storing of chemical drums together outside the building, direct to sunlight, without second layer of contentment and
recommendation: It needs review for overall improvement. without study of chemical compatibility of different chemicals.

observation: TFH (Thermic Fluid heater) testing records i.e. Oil testing report was not found.
recommendation: As per the Sub-rule 18 of Rule 68 – D of The Gujarat Factories Rules, 1963 Thermic fluid shall be tested by competent person for suitability at least in every three-month period. Such test shall include test for acidity, suspended matter, ash content, viscosity and flash point.

observation: Record of Pressure test of the heater coil of thermic fluid heater was not evident.
recommendation: As per rule 68 D (16) of The Gujarat Factories Rules, 1963 The heater coil shall be subjected to pressure test by competent person once at least in every 12 months. The test pressure shall not be less than twice the operating pressure. Record to be maintained.

observation: Thermic Fluid heater logbook was not evident.
recommendation: Thermic Fluid heater logbook to be maintain as per rule 68 D of The Gujarat Factories Rules, 1963

observation: Factory is having huge fire load in plant and fire hydrant system, fire extinguishers available but fire load calculation / fire adequacy report not evident.
recommendation: As per Rule 66 – A of The Factories Rules, 1963 fire load calculation shall be carried out to ensure adequacy of existing fire protection facility.
